title: asperula glomerata var. capitata
intro: Asperula glomerata var. capitata, also known as Woodruff, is a perennial herb belonging to the Rubiaceae family. It is a medicinal plant that has been traditionally used for its antiseptic, antiviral, and antibacterial properties, which can be attributed to the presence of compounds like asperuloside and iridoids. The health benefits of Woodruff include its ability to aid in digestion, reduce inflammation, and act as a natural remedy for colds and respiratory issues. The plant is typically prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion, and can also be used in herbal remedies such as salves and ointments.
